description: Error codes used when resetting a stream or sending STOP_SENDING. "The application SHOULD use a relevant error code when resetting or sending STOP_SENDING on any stream." Neither stack has this enum yet.

greasing.notes: Reserved for greasing. Not a single codepoint; excluded from the entries below.

entries.name: CANCELLED
entries.notes: The stream was cancelled by either endpoint. For subscriptions, PUBLISH_DONE may carry a more detailed status code.

entries.name: DELIVERY_TIMEOUT
entries.notes: A delivery timeout (§8) was exceeded for this stream.

entries.name: GOING_AWAY
entries.notes: The endpoint is rejecting this request because it has sent or received a GOAWAY.

entries.name: TOO_FAR_BEHIND
entries.notes: The corresponding subscription has exceeded the publisher's resource limits and is being terminated (§8).

entries.name: UNKNOWN_OBJECT_STATUS
entries.notes: In response to a FETCH, the publisher is unable to determine the status of the next Object in the requested range.

entries.name: EXPIRED_AUTH_TOKEN
entries.notes: The authorization token for the request has expired.

entries.name: EXCESSIVE_LOAD
entries.notes: The endpoint is overloaded and is resetting this stream.

entries.name: MALFORMED_TRACK
entries.notes: A relay publisher detected that the track was malformed (§2.4.2).
